macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Stable (STABLE) Token Surges 49% to $0.039 in 24 Hours
items.summary: Stable (STABLE) rallied 49.4% over the past 24 hours, reaching $0.039 with market cap expanding over 51% to $874 million, marking one of the top single-day gains among top 100 cryptocurrencies.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Exceed $118 Million, Long Traders Hit Hardest
items.summary: Crypto futures saw over $118 million in liquidations in the last 24 hours, primarily affecting long positions in Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana, signaling short-term bearish pressure.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $78,000 Amid Strong ETF Inflows and Low Exchange Reserves
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $77,600-$78,000 with a slight 0.67% decline, supported by over $335 million in spot ETF inflows over seven days and near-record low exchange reserves.

items.title: Global Crypto Market Cap Drops 1.62% to $2.51 Trillion as Majors Retreat
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market capitalization fell 1.62% to $2.51 trillion, with Bitcoin wobbling near $78,000, Ethereum at $2,300, over $200 million liquidated mostly in longs, while Dogecoin gained.

items.title: Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 1.47 Million BTC as ARK Invest Warns Bottom Not In
items.summary: Bitcoin whales added 69% more BTC (1.47 million) during Q1's 22% drawdown, but ARK Invest indicates the market bottom has not been reached yet.

items.title: BlackRock Seeks Bitcoin and Crypto Director Amid Institutional Interest
items.summary: BlackRock is hiring a Bitcoin and crypto director, signaling growing institutional interest in cryptocurrency markets.

items.title: Riot Platforms Sells 500 BTC, Deposits $38.95M to NYDIG
items.summary: Riot Platforms sold 500 BTC and deposited $38.95 million to NYDIG, potentially affecting Bitcoin market liquidity.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Nvidia's Strong Forecast Calms AI Bubble Jitters
items.summary: Nvidia shares jumped 5% in extended trading after a strong forecast, adding $220 billion in market value, easing concerns following an 8% drop amid a 1,200% surge over three years.

items.title: Google Opens Largest AI Infrastructure Hardware Center in Taiwan
items.summary: Google launched its biggest AI hardware engineering center outside the US in Taiwan, with President Lai Ching-te highlighting Google's long-term investment commitment.

items.title: Luma AI Raises $900 Million at $4 Billion Valuation
items.summary: Video generation platform Luma AI, led by Indian-American CEO Amit Jain, secured $900 million in funding, reaching a $4 billion valuation to scale its capital-intensive operations.

items.title: AMD, Cisco, and Saudi's Humain Launch AI Joint Venture with 100MW Data Center
items.summary: The new joint venture starts with a 100-megawatt data center in Saudi Arabia, supplying compute to Luma AI, marking its first major customer.

items.title: Brookfield Launches $100 Billion AI Infrastructure Program
items.summary: Brookfield announced a $100 billion AI infrastructure initiative, anchored by a new fund targeting $10 billion in equity commitments.

items.title: Nvidia and Menlo Micro Develop Chips to Speed AI Testing by 30-90%
items.summary: New switching chips from Nvidia and Menlo Micro accelerate AI chip testing, addressing production bottlenecks amid surging demand.

items.title: Meta Plans Layoffs of 8,000 Employees Amid AI Push
items.summary: Meta is set to lay off nearly 8,000 staff next month to offset rising AI infrastructure costs, with reports varying between 8,000 and 10% of workforce.

items.title: OpenAI New Model Adept at Improving AI; China's DeepSeek Releases New Model
items.summary: OpenAI announced a model skilled at enhancing AI systems, while China's DeepSeek launched a new model a year after its viral success.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie Shifts More Developers to Marathon Over Destiny 2
items.summary: Sources confirm that of Bungie's roughly 800 remaining employees, more are now working on Marathon than on Destiny 2, marking a shift in priorities within the studio.
items.source: YouTube - This is Not a Good Sign for Destiny

items.title: New Leaks Bring Bad News for Pokemon Winds & Waves and Gen 10
items.summary: Fresh leaks reveal troubling updates for Pokemon Winds & Waves, alongside confirmations of no Gen 10 news for the rest of 2026; multiple Pokemon games also received patches including Pokemon Home, Pokopia, and Champions.
items.source: YouTube - NEW LEAK UPDATES! MORE Bad News For Winds & Waves!

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Patch 12.0.5 Launch Faces Bugs, Timegating, and Price Hikes
items.summary: The release of Patch 12.0.5 has been plagued by numerous bugs, timegated content, increased subscription prices, and China-exclusive rewards.
items.source: YouTube - 12.0.5 Launch Problems - Bugs, Timegating & Subscription Changes

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SpaceX Plans In-House GPU Production Ahead of IPO
items.summary: SpaceX is planning substantial capital expenditures to manufacture its own GPUs as part of its push into AI infrastructure, tying into Elon Musk's Terafab vision with xAI and Tesla in Austin.

items.title: Elon Musk Announces Terafab AI Chip Project Using Intel's 14A Process
items.summary: Tesla will utilize Intel's 14A process for the Terafab chip project in Texas, producing chips for vehicles, Optimus robots, and AI data centers, with initial research fab construction underway at Giga Texas.

items.title: UK's BT Group Partners with Nscale for 14MW AI Data Centre Capacity
items.summary: BT Group is teaming up with Nscale to build up to 14 megawatts of AI data centre capacity across three UK sites using Nvidia infrastructure, enhancing AI capabilities for public sector and business customers.

items.title: Infosys Q4 Profit Jumps 21% YoY to Rs 8,501 Crore, Declares Rs 25/Share Dividend
items.summary: Infosys reported a 21% year-over-year profit increase to Rs 8,501 crore and 13% revenue growth in Q4, while approving Esops worth Rs 51.75 crore for CEO Salil Parekh and planning to hire 20,000 freshers in FY27.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon-Based Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to reshape personal computing, scaling the mobile OS to desktops and challenging Microsoft and Apple in the unified computing space.

items.title: Amazon Expands Alexa+ to the Web
items.summary: Amazon is bringing its Alexa+ AI assistant to the web, extending beyond devices toward agentic workflows and competing directly with rivals like ChatGPT and Google Gemini.

items.title: Netgear Receives Exemption from U.S. WiFi Router Ban
items.summary: Netgear has been granted an exemption from the U.S. ban on new foreign-made drones and WiFi routers, allowing continued operations amid restrictions.
